    rw [h₂]

  -- Relate the diagonal entry to the coordinate in WignerSpace

let diag_proj : WignerSpace n → ℝ := fun ω => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩
  -- Use the fact that the diagonal entries are independent and have the distribution ν
  have h₁ : HasLaw diag_proj ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- The diagonal entry is directly sampled from ν
    have h₂ : HasLaw diag_proj ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the property of the product measure to get the law of the projection
      have h₃ : HasLaw diag_proj ν (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        -- Apply the product measure property for the diagonal entry
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi_of_function
        <;> simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;> aesop
      -- Simplify the product measure to WignerMeasure
      simpa [WignerMeasure] using h₃
    exact h₂
  -- Show that the diagonal entry of the matrix is equal to the projection function
  have h₂ : (fun ω => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = diag_proj := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>
    (try simp_all [wignerMeasureDist, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ])
    <;>
    (try ring_nf)
    <;>
    (try norm_num)
    <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ])
    <;>
    (try omega)
  -- Substitute the equality to conclude the proof
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

      -- Prove that the coordinate projection has law ν

something else.

But perhaps the simplest approach is:
`Measure.map (fun ω => ω p) (Measure.pi f) = f p`, because the product measure is defined such that the coordinate `p` has measure `f p`.

Alternatively, in Lean, for `P = ∏' i, P_i`, then for any `i`, the projection onto `i` has measure `P_i`.

But perhaps not yet in Lean, for general products.

But perhaps we can use:
`(Measure.pi f).map (fun g : ι → ℝ => g p) = f p`.

This follows from the definition of product measures as the minimal measure containing all `∏ i ∈ s, A_i`, and the fact that the projection of `∏ i ∈ s ∪ {p}, A_i` when `s` doesn't contain `p` is `A_p`.

Alternatively, perhaps in Lean's `MeasureTheory.Measure.Prod`, there is a statement about projections for finite products.

But perhaps not for infinite products yet. Hmm.

Alternatively, perhaps we can use that the product measure is the same as the independent product, and use the `HasLaw.indep` lemma, to get that each coordinate is independent, so in particular, the margin is the corresponding factor. But perhaps not yet.

Alternatively, since Lean defines `WignerMeasure μ ν n` as `Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)`, perhaps we should find a general lemma that `(Measure.pi f).map (π i) = f i`, where `π i : (ι → ℝ) → ℝ` is the projection onto `i`.

But if not, we can try to use the fact that for any measurable set `S` in `ℝ`, `(Measure.pi f).map (π i) S = (Measure.pi f) (π i ⁻¹' S) = (Measure.pi f) {g : ι → ℝ | g i ∈ S}`. But perhaps we can't yet evaluate this unless we have more lemmas.

Alternatively, perhaps for the case of `ι = Index n`, which is finite, we can get that `Measure.pi` is the same as `Measure.prod`, and then perhaps we can use the `HasLaw.prod_mk` lemma to get that the projection is `f i`.

Hmm, perhaps not, since the `ι` is `Index n`, and for finite `ι`, `Measure.pi` is the same as `Measure.prod`, which is the product in the `Finset` sense (i.e., repeated product).

But perhaps Lean's `Measure.pi` is already the product over a `Fintype` as `Measure.prod`.

Actually, no, `WignerMeasure` uses `Measure.pi`, which is for any type `ι`, but perhaps in Lean, for `ι

    exact h₄
  exact h₂

have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω ↦ (if i = i then (ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) else if i < i then (ω (⟨(i, i), le_rfl⟩)) else (ω (⟨(i, i), le_rfl⟩)))) := by
      ext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> split_ifs <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      <;> try { contradiction }
      <;> try { linarith }
    rw [h₂]
    -- We need to show that the law of (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) is ν
    -- This follows from the fact that the diagonal entries are distributed according to ν
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (if i = i then (ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) else if i < i then (ω (⟨(i, i), le_rfl⟩)) else (ω (⟨(i, i), le_rfl⟩)))) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Since i = i, the first condition is true
      have h₄ : (i : Fin n) = (i : Fin n) := by rfl
      have h₅ : (fun ω ↦ (if i = i then (ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) else if i < i then (ω (⟨(i, i), le_rfl⟩)) else (ω (⟨(i, i), le_rfl⟩)))) = (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) := by
        funext ω
        simp [h₄]
        <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> aesop
      rw [h₅]
      -- Now we need to show that the law of (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) is ν
      -- This follows from the fact that the diagonal entries are distributed according to ν
      have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- Use the fact that the measure is constructed as a product measure
        have h₇ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_fst (p := (Index n) ×ₙ {1})
          <;> simp [WignerMeasure, Measure.pi, Measure.pi_def]
          <;> aesop
        -- Since (i, i) is a diagonal entry, the measure is ν
        have h₈ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) = ν := by
          simp [wignerMeasureDist]
          <;> aesop
        rw [h₈] at h₇
        exact h₇
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

funext ω
    rw [wignerMatrixMap]
    rw [wignerMatrixMap']
    simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, le_refl]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the fact that the coordinate projections are independent and have the specified distributions
    have h3 : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := by
      rfl
    rw [h3]
    -- Apply the property of the product measure to get the distribution of each coordinate
    have h4 : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) _
      := by
      apply HasLaw.measurable_fun
      <;> simp [Function.funext_iff]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          exact measurable_fst
        })
    -- Simplify the distribution for the diagonal entry
    have h5 : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist, le_refl]
      <;>
      aesop
    rw [h5] at h4
    exact h4
  exact h2

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> split_ifs <;> try contradiction <;>
      (try simp_all) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try {
        have h := ‹_›
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;> aesop
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try contradiction)
      })
    <;>
    (try aesop)
  
  have h2 : HasLaw (fun (x : WignerSpace n) ↦ x ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h : HasLaw (fun (x : WignerSpace n) ↦ x ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₁ : WignerMeasure μ ν n = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
      rw [h₁]
      -- Use the property of product measures to get the law of the coordinate function
      have h₂ : HasLaw (fun (x : WignerSpace n) ↦ x ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        apply hasLaw_apply
        <;> simp
      exact h₂
    exact h
  
  have h3 : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩ = ν := by
    simp [wignerMeasureDist]
    <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
  
  have h4 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h1]
    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun (x : WignerSpace n) ↦ x ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₅₁ : HasLaw (fun (x : WignerSpace n) ↦ x ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := h2
      have h₅₂ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp⟩ = ν := h3
      convert h₅₁ using 1
      <;> simp_all
    exact h₅
  
  exact h4

funext ω
    rw [wignerMatrixMap]
    rw [wignerMatrixMap']
    simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk]
    <;>

  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simpa using le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by

        (try contradiction) <;>

        (try linarith)
      })
    <;>

  have h_has_law : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by

    rw [h₂] at h₁
    exact h₁
  
  have h_final : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h_main] at *
    exact h_has_law
  
  exact h_final

have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      classical
      -- Use the fact that the diagonal entries have the same law as the diagonal distribution ν
      -- This follows from the construction of the Wigner measure and the properties of the projections
      -- Here we need to formalize the correspondence between the coordinates of the Wigner matrix and the product space
      -- For simplicity, we assume that the diagonal entries are directly mapped to the coordinates with the ν distribution
      -- This is a high-level sketch and needs to be refined in a formal proof
      have h₃ : Set.EqOn (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) (fun x => x ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (Set.univ : Set (Index n → ℝ)) := by
        intro ω _
        simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixEntryFunction, Matrix.of_apply]
        <;>
        (try cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try
          {
            by_cases h : (i : ℕ) = (i : ℕ) <;>
            simp_all [h] <;>
            aesop
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ] <;>
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            split_ifs <;>
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ] <;>
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ] <;>
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ] <;>
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distributions
      have h₄ : HasLaw (fun x : Index n → ℝ => x ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- The projection onto the diagonal entry has the ν distribution
        have h₅ : HasLaw (fun x : Index n → ℝ => x ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ((wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi
          <;> simp_all [WignerMeasure]
          <;> aesop
        -- Since ⟨(i, i), _⟩ is a diagonal entry, the measure is ν
        have h₆ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ = ν := by
          simp [wignerMeasureDist]
          <;>
          (try cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try
            {
              by_cases h : (i : ℕ) = (i : ℕ) <;>
              simp_all [h] <;>
              aesop
            }) <;>
          (try aesop)
        rw [h₆] at h₅
        exact h₅
      -- The function is equal almost everywhere, so the law is the same
      have h₇ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        have h₈ : Set.EqOn (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) (fun x => x ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (Set.univ : Set (Index n → ℝ)) := h₃
        have h₉ : HasLaw (fun x : Index n → ℝ => x ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := h₄
        have h₁₀ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          apply HasLaw.congr_ae
          · exact h₉
          · filter_upwards [ae_restrict_mem measurableSet_univ] with ω _
            rw [h₈ ω ‹_›]
        exact h₁₀
      exact h₇
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the given fact that the projection map is a law `ν`
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the i-th projection of the product measure
      apply Measure.pi_proj_has_law
      <;> simp [injEq]
    -- Simplify the measure for the diagonal entry
    have h₄ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist, le_refl]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  
  exact h₂

have h₂ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) := by
      apply measurable_entry
      exact wignerMatrixMapMeasurable n
    have h₃ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try {
        split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff] <;>
        (try { contradiction }) <;>
        (try { linarith }) <;>
        (try { aesop })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try { omega }) <;>
        (try { aesop })
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try { omega }) <;>
        (try { aesop })
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try { omega }) <;>
        (try { aesop })
      })
    rw [h₃]
    have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₅ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := rfl
      rw [h₅]
      -- Use the fact that the measure is defined as a product measure to extract the law of one coordinate
      have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_fst
      -- Show that the measure of the diagonal element is ν
      have h₇ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) = ν := by
        simp [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try { omega }) <;>
          (try { aesop })
        })
      rw [h₇] at h₆
      exact h₆
    exact h₄
  exact h₁
